FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class InputHooks

Namespace
FluentGpu.Hooks
Assembly
FluentGpu.Hooks.dll

A host-owned registration surface a tree-level concern can hook into without the host depending on it. The host bridges KeyPreview into the input dispatcher's pre-focus key hook, and runs AfterAnimations after the animation engine ticks but before recording. Read the host instance via UseContext(InputHooks.Current).

public sealed class InputHooks

CaretBlur

Stop blinking for an editor's text node (focus lost).

public Action<NodeHandle>? CaretBlur

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle>

CaretFocus

Arm the caret blinker for a (newly focused) editor's TEXT node; float = blink half-period ms (IPlatformTextInput.CaretBlinkMs).

public Action<NodeHandle, float>? CaretFocus

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle, float>

CaretReset

An edit happened: snap the caret visible and restart the blink phase.

public Action<NodeHandle>? CaretReset

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle>

Clipboard

The system clipboard (UI-thread only; IPlatformApp.Clipboard).

public IClipboard? Clipboard

Field Value

IClipboard

ClosePopupWindow

Release a leased popup window (hide + dispose the window and its swapchain).

public Action<int>? ClosePopupWindow

Field Value

Action<int>

Current 

public static readonly Context<InputHooks> Current

Field Value

Context<InputHooks>

FirstFocusableIn

First focusable node (tab order) within a subtree - the focus trap's initial-focus query. Host-wired to InputDispatcher.FirstFocusableIn.

public Func<NodeHandle, NodeHandle>? FirstFocusableIn

Field Value

Func<NodeHandle, NodeHandle>

FocusNode

Move keyboard focus to a node (host-wired to the dispatcher's SetFocus). visual=true draws the engine focus ring — keyboard-initiated moves (ItemsView arrow nav / typeahead) pass true; pointer-style moves pass false.

public Action<NodeHandle, bool>? FocusNode

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle, bool>

Fonts

The host's text seam — the SAME layout pipeline the renderer measures with, so editor hit-test/caret queries agree with drawn glyph positions exactly.

public IFontSystem? Fonts

Field Value

IFontSystem

GetFocus

The currently-focused node (host-wired to the dispatcher). An opening overlay captures it so the focus can be restored when the overlay closes (WinUI flyout focus-restoration).

public Func<NodeHandle>? GetFocus

Field Value

Func<NodeHandle>

GetNodeRect

Node → its laid-out absolute rect (window DIP) — host-wired to SceneStore.AbsoluteRect. The TitleBar control builds its region report from captured part handles in a layout effect.

public Func<NodeHandle, RectF>? GetNodeRect

Field Value

Func<NodeHandle, RectF>

GetWindowState

Pull the window placement (drives the max↔restore caption glyph). Null = standard frame (Normal).

public Func<WindowState>? GetWindowState

Field Value

Func<WindowState>

GetWorkArea

Window-DIP point → the containing MONITOR's work area translated into window-DIP space (the container windowed popups clamp against — WinUI FlyoutBase_Partial.cpp:3382-3392 useMonitorBounds). The host owns the DIP↔screen conversion (IPlatformWindow.ClientOriginPx + IPlatformApp.GetWorkArea).

public Func<Point2, RectF>? GetWorkArea

Field Value

Func<Point2, RectF>

ImeSetCaretRect

Position the IME candidate window: the caret rect in window DIP — the HOST converts to physical px (it owns the window scale) before calling IPlatformTextInput.SetCaretRectPx.

public Action<RectF>? ImeSetCaretRect

Field Value

Action<RectF>

IsWindowActive

Pull window activation (drives titlebar dimming). Null = always active.

public Func<bool>? IsWindowActive

Field Value

Func<bool>

KeyPreview

Return true to consume the key. Set by an open overlay; cleared when it closes.

public Func<int, bool>? KeyPreview

Field Value

Func<int, bool>

MoveFocusVisual

Move keyboard focus to a node WITH the focus visual (host-wired to the dispatcher's SetFocus(node, visual: true)) — the roving-focus seam for container controls (RadioButtons arrow navigation, RadioButtons.cpp MoveFocus), where WinUI shows the focus rect on the newly-focused item. Contrast RestoreFocus, which restores silently (overlay close).

public Action<NodeHandle>? MoveFocusVisual

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle>

OpenPopupWindow

Lease a popup WINDOW for an overlay subtree root (PopupOptions.ConstrainToRootBounds = false): the host creates the PAL popup window + its own swapchain and re-records the subtree into its own DrawList each frame (SceneRecorder root-override). Returns a token, or -1 when windowed popups are unavailable — callers fall back to constrained placement (WinUI's DoesPlatformSupportWindowedPopup gate).

public Func<NodeHandle, int>? OpenPopupWindow

Field Value

Func<NodeHandle, int>

OpenUri

Launch a URI in the OS default handler (IPlatformApp.OpenUri) — HyperlinkButton's WinUI NavigateUri step (Click first, then launch — HyperLinkButton_Partial.cpp:149-177). Host-wired in the AppHost ctor onto BOTH the host instance and the Current channel-default instance (static control factories have no component scope → no UseContext, so they reach the seam via the default). Null in a host-less tree → element construction/clicks never launch.

public Action<string>? OpenUri

Field Value

Action<string>

PopFocusScope

Remove the focus scope previously pushed for this root (order-independent - overlays can close out of stack order). Host-wired to InputDispatcher.RemoveFocusScope.

public Action<NodeHandle>? PopFocusScope

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle>

PushFocusScope

Push a dispatcher FOCUS SCOPE rooted at a node: Tab/Shift-Tab cycle inside its subtree until popped (WinUI TabFocusNavigation=Cycle - the ContentDialog/flyout focus trap). Host-wired to InputDispatcher.PushFocusScope.

public Action<NodeHandle>? PushFocusScope

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle>

RestoreFocus

Restore focus to a node (host-wired to the dispatcher). Used by an overlay on close.

public Action<NodeHandle>? RestoreFocus

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle>

SetPopupWindowBounds

Place a leased popup window: token + bounds in main-window DIP (the host converts to physical virtual-screen px and shows the window without activating it).

public Action<int, RectF>? SetPopupWindowBounds

Field Value

Action<int, RectF>

SetTitleBarRegions

Push the titlebar drag/button regions (array + count — the caller reuses ONE array across pushes, the host forwards regions.AsSpan(0, count) to IPlatformWindow.SetTitleBarRegions; push happens on titlebar relayout only, never per frame).

public Action<TitleBarRegion[], int>? SetTitleBarRegions

Field Value

Action<TitleBarRegion[], int>

TextInput

The focused window's IME/text-services seam (IPlatformWindow.TextInput): the focused editor registers its composition sink and flips SetEditable here. (Candidate-window placement goes through ImeSetCaretRect instead — it needs the host's DIP→px scale.)

public IPlatformTextInput? TextInput

Field Value

IPlatformTextInput

WindowBlurred

Set by the overlay host: the window lost activation → close every light-dismiss overlay (WinUI window-deactivation dismiss). Invoked from the dispatcher's WindowBlur via the host wiring.

public Action? WindowBlurred

Field Value

Action

WindowChromeEpoch

Bumped by the host on WindowFocus/WindowBlur/WindowStateChanged: the TitleBar reads it (subscribes) and pulls GetWindowState/IsWindowActive for the current values on re-render.

public Signal<int>? WindowChromeEpoch

Field Value

Signal<int>

WindowClose

Caption-button commands → IPlatformWindow.Minimize/ToggleMaximize/CloseWindow.

public Action? WindowClose

Field Value

Action

WindowMinimize

Caption-button commands → IPlatformWindow.Minimize/ToggleMaximize/CloseWindow.

public Action? WindowMinimize

Action

WindowToggleMaximize

Caption-button commands → IPlatformWindow.Minimize/ToggleMaximize/CloseWindow.

public Action? WindowToggleMaximize

Action

NotifyWindowBlur() 

public void NotifyWindowBlur()

Preview(int) 

public bool Preview(int key)

Parameters

key int

Returns

bool

RunAfterAnimations() 

public void RunAfterAnimations()

SetAfterAnimations(object, Action?)

Host phase 7 hook: after AnimEngine.Tick, before record/present. Tree-level systems with retained animation lifecycles (overlays) use this to finalize settled visuals without a one-frame post-present delay. Registered by owner so multiple tree systems can coexist without clobbering each other.

public void SetAfterAnimations(object owner, Action? action)

Parameters

owner object
action Action
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
